questions.question: What is Lazy Loading & Code Splitting and why is it important?
questions.answer: Lazy Loading & Code Splitting is a fundamental concept that Load resources on demand, dynamic imports, and intersection observer. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Lazy Loading & Code Splitting allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Lazy Loading & Code Splitting in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Lazy Loading & Code Splitting is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Lazy Loading & Code Splitting?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Lazy Loading & Code Splitting to a beginner?
questions.answer: Lazy Loading & Code Splitting is essentially about load resources on demand, dynamic imports, and intersection observer. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
